findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "search_docs" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Search the goldprice.dev FAQ corpus via BM25. Returns top-K matching questions + answers with relevance scores. Use before answering how-to / billing / auth que…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

description: Search the goldprice.dev FAQ corpus via BM25. Returns top-K matching questions + answers with relevance scores. Use before answering how-to / billing / auth questions so you cite the canonical text instead of guessing from training data.

description.name: get_forward_view
description.description: Returns the multi-horizon futures settlement curve for a commodity (XAU, XAG, HG). Every populated horizon up to one year forward. Useful for bot builders and traders seeing the futures-implied path. See https://goldprice.dev/methodology#forward-view-layers for per-layer math.

description.name: get_physical_price
description.description: Return the latest per-country physical dealer/retail gold prices — real quotes from dealers, mints, and bullion benchmarks, NOT spot-derived arithmetic — with premium_over_spot_bps as a first-class field per quote. `country` is an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (e.g. ID, IN, AE, JP). Requires the Physical

description.name: get_surface
description.description: Return the market-implied probability surface for gold or silver — what the options market (GLD/SLV chains) and the prediction markets (Kalshi, Polymarket) jointly imply about where the metal is going. `metal` is xau or xag; optional `horizon` is an ISO date from the expiries index. findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "search_docs" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Search the goldprice.dev FAQ corpus via BM25. Returns top-K matching questions + answers with relevance scores. Use before answering how-to / billing / auth que…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_metadata · Private data: get_metadata, get_historical · Public sink: get_forward_view.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_metadata · Private data: get_metadata, get_historical · Public sink: get_forward_view

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
